author: Ben Halpern
message: fix(spec): wait for body[data-loaded] in editor system specs to avoid flakes (#23596)

author: Jonathan Gottfried
message:
Use spec-compliant identity fields on CDP calls (#23594)

* Use spec-compliant identity fields on CDP calls

Identity now follows the Segment spec instead of using email as the
userId: every call carries a stable anonymous_id ("dev:<user id>"),
and user_id is set to the canonical MLH user id once the account is
linked (resolved at send time; destructive events fall back to the
payload's pre-destruction capture). Sending both ids stitches the
pre-link anonymous history onto the person downstream. Email is no
longer an identity field — it travels in properties only — and GDPR
deletion events now deliver even for accounts without an email.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

* Pass track attributes as a plain hash

analytics-ruby's #track goes through method_missing and expects a single
attributes hash; the keyword splat only worked via Ruby 3's fallback
conversion. Passing the hash directly is equivalent and unambiguous.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

author: Ben Halpern
message: fix: modernize tag landing page sidebar design and button alignment (#23593)

author: Jonathan Gottfried
message:
Fix bulk identities handling of same-batch duplicate users (#23592)

The per-user identity preload was never updated after a create, so a
batch repeating the same user_id either mis-reported an already_linked
row as a conflict (same uid) or failed the whole request with a 422
(different uid, via the model's per-user uniqueness validation) —
violating the endpoint's documented per-item isolation. Record created
identities in the preload so same-batch repeats resolve like
pre-existing rows.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

author: Jonathan Gottfried
message:
Restore bulk admin API endpoint for linking MLH identities (#23590)

* Add a bulk admin API endpoint for linking mlh identities

The Core -> DEV reverse-link seed covers ~2M accounts; one HTTP call
per identity would take days against the API rate limits. POST
/api/admin/users/identities/bulk links up to 1,000 identities per
request with per-item results (created / already_linked / conflict /
not_found / invalid) so one bad row never fails the batch, reusing the
same fail-closed conflict semantics as the single-identity endpoint,
with one audit entry per batch.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

* Address review: batch preloads, malformed entries, missing locales

- Preload users, per-user identities, and taken uids in three queries
  per batch instead of three per row; the taken-uid set is mutated as
  rows link so same-batch duplicate uids conflict like pre-existing
  ones, and the RecordNotUnique rescue still covers concurrent writers.
- Non-object identities entries (strings, nulls) now report per-item
  status invalid instead of raising and failing the whole batch.
- Add the invalid_identities key to fr/pt locales (fixes the i18n spec).
- Set the mlh omniauth mock file-wide so the identity factory does not
  depend on spec order.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

author: Jonathan Gottfried
message:
Suppress CDP newsletter events for admin-API notification settings writes (#23589)

The endpoint carries MLH Core's own consent state (its dev-newsletter
opt-outs and opt-ins). Emitting user_newsletter_subscribed/unsubscribed
for those writes made Core consume its own push back as a user consent
change: a Core-side global unsubscribe would permanently overwrite the
user's underlying DEV list preference, so lifting the global
unsubscribe could no longer restore it. Core-originated writes are now
silent on the CDP; genuine user toggles (settings page, one-click
unsubscribe, onboarding, Mailchimp webhook) still emit. Mailchimp sync
and base_email_eligible callbacks still run for admin writes.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

author: Jonathan Gottfried
message:
Emit user_merged instead of user_gdpr_deleted when admins merge users (#23580)

Users::DeleteWorker assumed every deletion is a GDPR erasure: it created
a GDPRDeleteRequest and emitted user_gdpr_deleted to the CDP even when
Moderator::MergeUser deletes the merged-away row — making MLH Core treat
an account merge as an erasure (destroying data that just moved to the
kept account) and leaving admins a bogus purge reminder.

The worker now takes an explicit deletion reason (default "gdpr") and
only performs the GDPR side effects for real erasures. MergeUser passes
"merge" and emits user_merged from the kept user with
merged_forem_user_id so Core can merge the two accounts.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

author: Jonathan Gottfried
message:
Add an admin API endpoint for updating user notification settings (#23585)

* Add an admin API endpoint for updating user notification settings

Lets MLH Core push consent changes (its dev-newsletter opt-outs) back
onto the DEV account's local email_newsletter flag, which DEV's own
senders key off. Writes through the model so the normal callbacks fire
(Mailchimp sync, and the CDP newsletter events once enabled); audited
per change; only email_newsletter is permitted for now.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

* Address review: explicit wrapper handling and missing locales

- A missing notification_setting wrapper now returns the admin API's
  invalid_notification_settings error_code instead of a generic
  ParameterMissing 422, with a regression spec.
- Add the invalid_notification_settings key to fr/pt locales.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

author: Ben Halpern
message: Strip trailing whitespace from event descriptions in liquid embed (#23588)

author: Jonathan Gottfried
message:
Deliver user_gdpr_deleted despite the destroyed user row (#23587)

* Deliver user_gdpr_deleted despite the destroyed user row

The CDP adapter resolves emails by querying the users table at send
time, so the one event that fires after the row is destroyed —
user_gdpr_deleted — resolved zero emails and was silently dropped
before reaching Core. When no row matches, fall back to the payload's
own email (the DeleteWorker builds it from the in-memory attributes).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

* Address review: gate the payload-email fallback to destructive events

An unrestricted fallback meant a straggler event (e.g. user_updated)
for a just-deleted user could deliver via the stale payload address —
potentially resurrecting state after a GDPR erasure. Only
user_gdpr_deleted may use the payload email; everything else must
resolve a live row, with a negative regression spec pinning that.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

* Move DESTRUCTIVE_EVENTS up with the other constants (rubocop ClassStructure)

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

author: Ben Halpern
message: Fix markdown parser HTML leak inside event liquid embed button (#23584)

author: Jonathan Gottfried
message:
Widen DEV → Core CDP sync coverage: newsletter, email, confirmation, engagement (#23579)

* Widen DEV → Core CDP sync coverage: newsletter, email, confirmation, engagement

The sync previously only emitted on create and profile edits, so Core
never learned about:
- email_newsletter toggles (settings page, one-click unsubscribe links,
  onboarding, Mailchimp webhook) → user_newsletter_subscribed/unsubscribed
- the actual users.email swap (reconfirmable lands it outside profile
  edits) and email confirmation → user_updated now also fires on
  email/confirmed_at changes, and the payload carries confirmed_at
- the Core user id from the mlh identity → payload carries mlh_user_id,
  and linking a new mlh identity touches profile_updated_at
- ongoing activity → throttled user_engaged (at most one per user per
  week via a cache key) keeps Core engaged_at fresh
- invited accounts: user_created now waits for registered: true

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

* Address review: atomic engagement throttle, presence-ping coverage, spec timing

- engagement_emission_due? now takes the throttle slot atomically via
  Rails.cache.write(..., unless_exist: true) (matches notification.rb /
  send_user_digest_worker.rb idiom)
- update_presence! bypasses callbacks (update_column), so it now emits
  the throttled user_engaged explicitly; throttle check runs before the
  role query so hot presence pings stay cheap
- authenticator spec asserts profile_updated_at with be_within of
  Time.current instead of a 1.minute.ago comparison

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

* Close sync-bypass gaps: admin email edits, magic-link confirm, bots, dead letters

Drift-audit follow-ups:
- The three admin email paths (member manager update_email and
  confirm_pending_email, admin API update_email) write via
  update_columns, which bypasses the Trackable after_commit — emit
  user_updated explicitly so Core learns the new address.
- Magic-link sign-in confirms the email via update_column — emit
  user_updated so Core learns the identifier is verified.
- Bot accounts (community_bot/member_bot) no longer emit any sync
  events; the Core backfill excluded bots but CommunityBots::CreateBot
  accounts (registered: true) were slipping into the live sync.
- Trackable::DispatchWorker events that exhaust their 5 retries now
  notify Honeybadger instead of dead-lettering silently.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01BbriEeFARfLvUDKbH3WYAt

author: Jonathan Gottfried
message:
Stop writing users.mlh_username on MyMLH sign-in (#23576)

The MLH ↔ Core link lives on the identity row (uid = Core user id), so
the column stays nil instead of mirroring the OAuth nickname. New MyMLH
signups get a generator-random initial username (picked properly in
onboarding), and the banished-username guard no longer matches on MLH
nicknames — both documented in the updated specs. The column itself is
left in place; dropping it requires guarding the shared username-field
machinery and can happen separately if ever worth it.

author: Jonathan Gottfried
message:
Emit DEV moderation events (suspend/spam/banish) to the Customer.io CDP (#23574)

* Emit DEV moderation events to the Customer.io CDP

Suspending, spam-flagging, or banishing a user (and the reversals) now
emits user_suspended / user_spam_flagged / user_banished /
user_unsuspended / user_spam_unflagged through the existing Trackable
pipeline, so MLH Core can scope the DEV newsletter and prune bot
Customer.io profiles. Emission goes through add_role/remove_role (the
single choke point for every moderation path) and only fires on actual
role transitions; banish also emits explicitly with the pre-banish
username. Same gates as the rest of the DEV → Core sync:
customerio_cdp_enabled + the dev_core_user_sync feature flag.

* Emit user_gdpr_deleted to the DEV → Core sync on account deletion

Users::DeleteWorker is the single choke point for both self-service and
admin deletions (it already records the GDPRDeleteRequest), so the
event is emitted there after the destroy. Core uses it to erase the
DEV-derived data it holds — and to delete DEV-only user records and
their Customer.io profiles entirely.

* Suppress the banish-internal user_suspended emission

Banish grants the suspended role via handle_user_status, which the new
add_role override would emit as user_suspended alongside user_banished.
The two events race through the CDP: if user_suspended lands second,
Core's moderation marker downgrades from banished to suspended after
the CIO prune already ran. Wrapping the role grant in
User.skip_trackable_events makes banish a single user_banished event.

author: Ashley Childress
message:
Fix fullscreen code block exit control on touch and non-hover devices (#23543)

* test: drop e2e seeds for removed Listing models

- ListingCategory no longer exists, so seed load crashed with a NameError
- unblocks all seeded end-to-end runs, not just new specs

Assisted-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: patch cypress-rails transactions for Rails 8

- Rails 8 removed ConnectionPool#connection and #lock_thread=
- pin and unpin pools the way Rails 8 transactional fixtures do
- upstream fix (testdouble/cypress-rails#165) remains unmerged

Generated-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: keep fullscreen code block exit control reachable

- derive open state from the DOM so clicks never desync from the overlay
- raise the overlay above the fixed header and show the panel without hover
- release the scroll lock and listeners when a soft navigation interrupts
- cover exit, Escape, occlusion, and back/forward flows in jest and cypress

Closes #23535
Generated-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: always unsubscribe connection subscriber on rollback

- move unsubscribe ahead of the blank-pools early return so an empty
  writing pool list cannot leak the subscriber into later runs
- nil the handle after unsubscribing to make repeat rollbacks inert

Generated-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: pin page title to stop faker collision flake

- Faker::Book.title draws from a small pool, so the let! page and post
  params can roll the same title
- find_by(title:) then matched the pre-existing page and failed the
  nil expectation in api/v1 pages specs

Generated-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Ashley Childress
message:
Mask page views under 25 on /manage to match the dashboard (#23549)

The manage page's Statistics card rendered the raw page_views_count
column, surfacing a misleading "0" for low-traffic posts. Counts at or
below 25 are unreliable by design (1-in-10 anonymous sampling, author
views excluded), which is why the posts dashboard masks them as "< 25".
Render the same masked value on /manage using the existing
views.dashboard.article.views.lt_25 translation.

Closes #23542

Generated-by: Claude Fable 5

Co-authored-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
